content:
How to Apply to Durban University of Technology (DUT)

Thinking about studying at Durban University of Technology (DUT)? Based in Durban, KwaZulu-Natal, DUT is a University of Technology established in 2002. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: DUT Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameDurban University of Technology (DUT)
TypeUniversity of Technology
LocationSteve Biko Campus and ML Sultan Campus (Durban); Indumiso Campus (Pietermaritzburg)
Year Founded2002
Application Portalhttps://www.dut.ac.za/prospective_students/how_to_apply/
Application FeeR100
Applications OpenApril 1
Applications CloseSeptember 30
Minimum APS20 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone031 373 2000
Contact Emailadmissions@dut.ac.za

Why Choose DUT?

Leading university of technology in KwaZulu-Natal.

  • Formed in 2002 by merging ML Sultan Technikon and Technikon Natal
  • Located in central Durban with easy access to the city's harbour and business district
  • DUT's Faculty of Health Sciences includes unique programmes in Chiropractic and Homeopathy
  • Strong in biomedical technology, serving Durban's major hospitals

Faculties and Programmes at DUT

DUT offers qualifications across 6 faculties:

  • Accounting & Informatics
  • Applied Sciences
  • Arts & Design
  • Engineering & the Built Environment
  • Health Sciences
  • Management Sciences

Popular programmes include: National Diploma Engineering, BCom Accounting, Industrial Design, Nursing, Biomedical Technology.

APS Requirements for DUT

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at DUT is 20 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: 20–28 depending on faculty

DUT requires a minimum APS of 20 for Diplomas and 24+ for Degrees. Health Sciences programmes like Chiropractic and Radiography have stricter requirements. DUT also offers advanced diplomas for National Diploma graduates.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to DUT

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.dut.ac.za. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the DUT online application portal at https://www.dut.ac.za/prospective_students/how_to_apply/. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of R100.

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from DUT, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for DUT

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: April 1
  • Application Deadline: September 30
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at DUT

Studying at DUT can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • NSFAS
  • DUT Financial Aid
  • eThekwini Municipality Bursary
  • KZN Department of Health Bursaries

NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme): If your combined household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

Preparing for DUT While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at DUT to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to DUT, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing DUT With Other Universities

Not sure if DUT is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorDUTTypical Alternative
TypeUniversity of TechnologyTraditional University / UoT
LocationDurbanVaries
Minimum APS2020–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeR100R100–R400
NSFAS EligibleYesYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to DUT

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to DUT.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet DUT's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.
keywords: Durban University of Technology (DUT) how to apply
quickAnswer: To apply to Durban University of Technology (DUT), visit https://www.dut.ac.za/prospective_students/how_to_apply/, pay the R100 application fee, and submit your documents before September 30. Minimum APS is 20 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to DUT?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at DUT is 20 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (20–28 depending on faculty). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the DUT website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for DUT?
faqs.answer: Applications for DUT typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.dut.ac.za/prospective_students/how_to_apply/ to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for DUT?
faqs.answer: The application fee at DUT is R100. Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the DUT website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does DUT accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, DUT is an accredited NSFAS institution. If your household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding which can cover tuition, accommodation, meals, transport, and a living allowance. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to DUT?
faqs.answer: For your application to DUT, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
